channel.title: Side Project on cekrem.github.io
channel.description: Recent content in Side Project on cekrem.github.io

item.title: codimg: the code block is the URL
item.description: Leaving my general dislike for Webflow aside for now, let’s at least agree on one specific thing: it is terrible at code. We have markdown.enso.no for the actual writing – you draft in markdown, it gives you something you can paste straight into Webflow’s WYSIWYG editor – and that pipeline is fine, right up until there’s a code block in it. Then it falls apart. Smart quotes where you typed straight ones, your indentation quietly flattened, the whole thing landing in the body font like it’s a line from a brochure. There are workarounds (an embed block, or an iframe if you really hate yourself), and not one of them is nice.

item.title: I made learnelm.dev
item.description:
Quick Friday post, mostly to give you this: learnelm.dev.

It’s a curated link directory. Nothing fancy. Just the stuff I’d actually send a friend if they asked me where to start with Elm – the official guide, the blog posts I keep linking to, talks that are worth an hour of your time, the playgrounds, the books.

If something’s missing – and something is definitely missing, as I haven’t spent a lot of time on this – PRs are more than welcome. I’d much rather have your favorite Elm resource on there than not.

item.title: GreenType: a beautifully coded terrible idea
item.description:
I built a keyboard that does basically nothing (except cool thock sounds), and it was super fun.

When you start typing, you and everyone else on the page hears a Cherry MX Blue sample (a different sound per key, because I went further down that rabbit hole than I should have). A trail of recent keystrokes fades across the screen. A global counter ticks up. That’s the app.

Someone in the Elm Slack called it “a beautifully coded terrible idea,” which feels about right.
